question.text: What happens if a person dies who was liable to pay a penalty under SCRA?

answer.text: The legal representative is liable to pay the penalty to the same extent as the deceased, but only if the penalty was imposed before death
The penalty is automatically waived upon death
The legal representative must pay double the original penalty
The penalty becomes a charge on the estate regardless of when it was imposed

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23JC states that where a person dies, the legal representative shall be liable to pay any sum which the deceased would have been liable to pay. However, the legal representative is liable only if the penalty was imposed before the death of the deceased person. **The Trap:** The liability doesn't automatically waive upon death, but it also doesn't extend to penalties imposed after death. The timing of penalty imposition is the critical factor. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This provision ensures penalty obligations survive death but limits legal representative liability to pre-death penalties — a balance between enforcement and fairness.
